message.content:
I'm implementing US-040 for Marketify вҖ” a creator-facing "campaign detail" screen at `app/(creator)/listing/[id].tsx` inside an Expo app. The story's acceptance criteria (from `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/scripts/ralph/prd.json` story id US-040):

- fetches via the `get-listing-detail` edge function
- renders title, description, price (display-only), pre/post conditions, sample videos as TikTok/IG links
- If eligible: ButtonPrimary 'Apply'; if not: greyed CTA with bullet list of failed reasons

I need a focused summary so I can implement cleanly without re-reading half the repo. Please return:

1. **`get-listing-detail` edge function contract** вҖ” request shape, response shape, error codes. It was built in US-039 and lives at `supabase/functions/get-listing-detail/index.ts`. Include the exact TypeScript types I'd write on the client. In particular, the shape of `conditions` (spec says a single `ListingCondition[]` array split by `kind`) and `eligibility.failed_conditions` shape.

2. **Campaign detail screen spec** вҖ” `docs/design.md` section for the creator campaign detail layout (typography, spacing, hero card treatment, conditions listing). Include the sample-videos treatment (docs likely say they render as links to TikTok/IG).

3. **Relevant existing primitives I should reuse** вҖ” ButtonPrimary (disabled state), StatusPill (if used for eligibility badge), any existing "ink-bordered card" container, `src/components/primitives/*` worth knowing about. Quote the props + variants.

4. **Routing pattern** вҖ” how do expo-router dynamic segments work in this repo (look at any existing `[param].tsx` under `app/`)? How does `useLocalSearchParams` get typed? Is there an existing back-button / header pattern for creator-group child routes?

5. **Supabase client invocation pattern** вҖ” specifically how authed edge-function calls are made from client code (I expect something like `supabase.functions.invoke('get-listing-detail', { body: { listing_id } })`). Cite any relevant code (US-033 signup lister, or US-035 profile screen are good examples).

6. **Navigation back to discover** вҖ” since `/(creator)/discover` (US-038) isn't implemented yet, I'll need to navigate to the detail screen via a temporary dev-link (e.g. from the creator profile screen, or via deep link). What's the recommended pattern for dev-only navigation helpers in this repo (I recall something about `__DEV__` gating)?

Keep the response under 600 words. Cite file paths + line numbers where useful. Don't implement anything вҖ” just return the research briefing.

Relevant doc files: `docs/design.md` (visual language), `docs/tech-architecture.md` (В§5.4 edge function spec, В§6.3 eligibility).
Relevant code files: `supabase/functions/get-listing-detail/index.ts`, `src/components/primitives/*`, `app/(creator)/profile.tsx`, `app/(creator)/_layout.tsx`, `src/design/{tokens,typography,motion}.ts`.

1	# Marketify вҖ” UI/UX Design Document
2	
3	*Mobile app for the creator marketing industry. Expo + React Native + Reanimated + Supabase.*
4	
5	---
6	
7	## 1. Design Language
8	
9	### 1.1 Chosen Style
10	
11	**Neubrutalism** as the primary language, with disciplined restraint for mobile comfort.
12	
13	**Why it fits Marketify:**
14	- The marketplace/directory pattern (Vibrant & Block-based) is the top match; Neubrutalism is its most purposeful expression вҖ” bold borders, hard offset shadows, flat pop colors.
15	- The pattern rewards scannability вҖ” hard black outlines on CampaignCards make bounties feel like physical "cards on a board."
16	- WCAG AAA by construction (high contrast), Excellent performance вҖ” critical for mid-tier Android.
17	- Differentiated. TikTok is dark-glassmorphic, Instagram is quiet gradient, every campaign-management tool (Grin, Aspire) is corporate-blue SaaS. Neubrutalism carves out a clear creator-native identity.
18	
19	**Disciplines to keep it mobile-friendly (not a desktop stunt):**
20	- Borders are 2px, not 4px.
21	- Shadows are 3px offset max, never 6вҖ“8px.
22	- Corner radius is 12px on cards, 999px on pills вҖ” not 0. Pure 0 feels brittle on touch.
23	- No intentionally "broken" layouts. Brutalism's anti-design aesthetic is skipped in favor of its craft elements only.
24	
25	### 1.2 Color Palette
26	
27	Base palette anchored on the **"Creator Economy Platform"** colors (pink + orange CTA), with semantic statuses added.
28	
29	| Role | Token | Hex | Usage |
30	|---|---|---|---|
31	| Primary | `--primary` | `#EC4899` | Brand, active tab icon, primary surfaces, logo mark |
32	| Primary Soft | `--primary-soft` | `#FDF2F8` | Primary-tinted backgrounds, selected chip bg |
33	| Primary Deep | `--primary-deep` | `#831843` | Primary text on soft bg, pressed states |
34	| Accent (CTA) | `--cta` | `#F97316` | All primary action buttons, "Apply", "Submit", "Create Campaign" |
35	| Accent Deep | `--cta-deep` | `#9A3412` | CTA pressed state |
36	| Ink (text) | `--ink` | `#0F172A` | All body and heading text, hard borders |
37	| Ink-70 | `--ink-70` | `#475569` | Secondary text, captions |
38	| Ink-40 | `--ink-40` | `#94A3B8` | Tertiary / disabled text |
39	| Surface | `--surface` | `#FFFFFF` | Card bodies |
40	| Canvas | `--canvas` | `#FFF9F2` | App background (warm off-white вҖ” matches orange CTA undertone) |
41	| Hairline | `--hairline` | `#E5E7EB` | Dividers inside cards |
42	| **Success (approved)** | `--success` | `#16A34A` | Approved submissions, approved applications, eligible checks |
43	| Success Soft | `--success-soft` | `#DCFCE7` | Approved pill bg |
44	| **Danger (rejected)** | `--danger` | `#DC2626` | Rejected submissions, rejected applications |
45	| Danger Soft | `--danger-soft` | `#FEE2E2` | Rejected pill bg, ineligibility warning |
46	| **Warning (pending)** | `--warning` | `#F59E0B` | Pending status, waiting-for-review states |
47	| Warning Soft | `--warning-soft` | `#FEF3C7` | Pending pill bg |
48	| **Cancelled** | `--cancelled` | `#6B7280` | Auto-cancelled applications |
49	| Cancelled Soft | `--cancelled-soft` | `#F3F4F6` | Cancelled pill bg |
50	| Shadow | `--shadow` | `#0F172A` | Neubrutalist hard shadow (always ink, never tinted) |
51	
52	**Contrast targets (measured against `--canvas` `#FFF9F2`):**
53	- Body text (`--ink` `#0F172A`): **17.8:1** вҖ” passes WCAG AAA.
54	- Secondary text (`--ink-70` `#475569`): **7.9:1** вҖ” passes AAA.
55	- Primary brand pink (`--primary` `#EC4899`) on canvas: **~3.3:1** вҖ” passes WCAG AA for large text (18pt+ or 14pt bold) and UI components, but **does not clear AAA**. Primary pink is therefore **never used for body text** вҖ” only for brand marks, large display accents, active-tab icons (paired with a text label), and soft-tinted backgrounds. Where pink needs to carry meaning on light backgrounds at body size, we use `--primary-deep` `#831843` (contrast ~11:1, AAA).
56	- CTA orange (`--cta` `#F97316`) is a background color under black label text (17.8:1), so the button itself is AAA; the orange is never used as foreground text.
57	
58	**Dark mode:** deferred for MVP. Neubrutalism translates cleanly (invert canvas вҶ’ `#0B0B0F`, keep shadow as a white 1px border with a `rgba(0,0,0,0.4)` offset), but it doubles visual QA.
59	
60	### 1.3 Font Pairing
61	
62	**"Startup Bold"** вҖ” `Clash Display` for headings, `Satoshi` for body. Both via `expo-font` (Fontshare CDN or self-host). Fallbacks: `Outfit` for headings, `Rubik` for body.
63	
64	| Role | Font | Weight | Size (mobile) |
65	|---|---|---|---|
66	| Display (screen titles, campaign titles) | Clash Display | 600вҖ“700 | 28 / 22 |
67	| Heading | Clash Display | 600 | 20 |
68	| Subhead | Satoshi | 600 | 17 |
69	| Body | Satoshi | 500 | 15 |
70	| Caption | Satoshi | 500 | 13 |
71	| Micro (pills, chip labels) | Satoshi | 700 | 11 (uppercase, `letter-spacing: 0.08em`) |
72	| Monospace (URLs, handles @xxx) | JetBrains Mono | 500 | 14 |
73	
74	**Usage rules:**
75	- Display font is *only* for titles and hero metric numbers. Never stack two Clash Display elements in the same card.
76	- User-generated content (campaign descriptions) bumps to 16px.
77	- Line-height: 1.55 body, 1.15 display.
78	- Line-length: max 65 characters вҶ’ content width caps at ~340px on a 390px screen.
79	
80	**Dynamic Type / system font scale.** Honor iOS Dynamic Type and Android font scale up to **130%** on all text вҖ” all sizes above scale proportionally via `allowFontScaling={true}` (RN default). **Cap at 130%** to protect Neubrutalism's fixed line-heights (1.15 display) and the 340px content-width cap, both of which break visually past that threshold (letters start touching; cards overflow gutters). Above 130% we clamp the scale factor in a top-level `Text.defaultProps.maxFontSizeMultiplier = 1.3` setting вҖ” the OS-level preference is still reflected up to the cap, just not beyond it. For users who need more, the v2 accessibility settings screen will expose a "Full size-up (may break layout)" escape hatch. Pills, status chips, and tab-bar labels use `allowFontScaling={false}` because their containers are fixed вҖ” they're UI chrome, not content.
81	
82	### 1.4 Motion Language (Reanimated)
83	
84	**Spring presets** (define in `animations/springs.ts`):
85	```
86	soft:    { damping: 20, stiffness: 180, mass: 1 }   // cards, chips
87	snappy:  { damping: 18, stiffness: 260, mass: 1 }   // tab switch, pill transitions
88	bouncy:  { damping: 12, stiffness: 220, mass: 1 }   // status change celebration
89	```
90	
91	**Timing presets:**
92	```
93	micro:  150ms  ease-out     // press feedback
94	short:  220ms  ease-out     // enter/exit
95	medium: 320ms  cubic-bezier(0.2, 0.8, 0.2, 1)  // screen transitions
96	```
97	
98	**Motion rules:**
99	1. **Screen transitions** вҖ” stack push uses native `slide_from_right` (expo-router default). Modals use `modal` presentation with a spring slide-up (snappy).
100	2. **Press feedback** вҖ” every pressable scales to 0.97 on `pressIn` (spring.soft), returns on `pressOut`. Use `Pressable` + `useAnimatedStyle` with `useSharedValue`. Never use `TouchableOpacity`.
101	3. **Status change (pending вҶ’ approved/rejected)** вҖ” StatusPill morphs color with a 320ms color interpolation + one-shot bouncy scale (1 вҶ’ 1.12 вҶ’ 1). Approved gets confetti-lite: 8 small squares in primary/cta/success burst upward from the pill, fade over 800ms. Rejected gets a gentle left-right shake (3 cycles, Вұ4px, 120ms each). Both respect `prefers-reduced-motion`. **Deep-link arrivals** (user tapped the push notification and the Submission Detail screen is the first screen they land on) вҖ” the celebration anchor is the StatusPill inside the just-mounted screen, triggered 220ms after mount (after the screen-slide settles) so the burst reads as a response to the arrival, not a hanging-over-from-last-screen artifact. If the screen mounts with `status` already `approved` (i.e. the user arrived via deep link, not via an in-app realtime update), we still fire the celebration exactly once, gated by a `celebration_seen_for_submission_id` entry in MMKV so revisits don't re-trigger.
102	4. **Pull-to-refresh on metrics** вҖ” custom refresh control. Vertical "pull" reveals a Clash Display counter that rolls up (`withTiming` + numerical interpolation). On success, new value "slams" into place with bouncy spring and `вң“` fades in. Delta shows `+1.2K` in success green for 1.5s.
103	5. **Card interactions (CampaignCard)** вҖ” on press, the card drops its hard shadow (translate `3px,3px` вҶ’ `0,0`) and moves `translateX/Y(2px,2px)` to simulate a physical press. Spring.soft.
104	6. **Eligibility check animation** вҖ” tap "Apply" вҶ’ criteria animate in a staggered list (each row fades + slides 8px from left, 60ms stagger). Passing conditions show a green check drawing in (SVG stroke-dashoffset, 280ms). Failing conditions shake once and fade to muted.
105	7. **Tab bar** вҖ” active tab icon morphs from `strokeWidth=1.5` outline to `strokeWidth=2.5` + filled enclosed areas via a paired ship-in-repo SVG (crossfaded, 180ms). Active indicator is a 4px bar under the icon (spring.snappy).
106	8. **FlatList entries** вҖ” on first mount, cards stagger in 40ms apart (translateY 16 вҶ’ 0, opacity 0 вҶ’ 1). Skip for subsequent renders. **Skip on screen re-entry within the same session** вҖ” a `useFocusEffect`-tracked `hasMountedOnce` ref gates the stagger so re-entering Feed from a pushed detail screen (or switching tabs back) doesn't replay the animation. Pull-to-refresh does *not* re-stagger either; new rows fade in individually (`LinearTransition` via `react-native-reanimated`'s layout animation API, 180ms). Full app relaunches reset the ref вҖ” the stagger is a first-impression-per-session flourish, not a per-mount one.
107	
108	**Performance:** all worklets, `runOnUI` for imperative runs. No `Animated` API. Gestures via `react-native-gesture-handler` + Reanimated v3.
109	
110	**Reduced motion:** wrap spring helpers in `useReducedMotion()`. Replace springs with `withTiming(value, { duration: 0 })` or short 120ms fade. Confetti and shake become no-ops; color transitions remain.

113	
114	| Attribute | Decision |
115	|---|---|
116	| Corners | Cards 12px, buttons 10px, pills 999px, inputs 10px, images 8px. |

119	| Elevation | Two tiers: "flat" (no shadow) and "lifted" (hard shadow). No z-stacking beyond modal overlay. |

Tab bar uses **stroke-weight toggle**, not filled variants вҖ” Lucide doesn't ship filled glyphs for every icon we need (`video`, `megaphone`, `inbox`). Inactive tab: `strokeWidth={1.5}`; active tab: `strokeWidth={2.5}` + fills the glyph's enclosed areas (e.g. compass needle) via a paired `fill` SVG we ship ourselves for the 4 creator + 4 lister tab icons. No emoji. |

221	| Tab | Icon | Label | Stack root |
222	|---|---|---|---|
223	| Dashboard | layout-dashboard | Home | `/(lister)/dashboard` |

229	
230	**Inbox model вҖ” relationship to В§2.3 IA.** В§2.3 lists Applications Inbox and Submissions Inbox as *campaign-scoped* routes (`/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/applications` and `/(lister)/campaigns/[id]/submissions`) because review always happens in-context for a specific campaign. The **Inbox tab** (`/(lister)/inbox`) is a cross-campaign roll-up вҖ” same underlying data, different entry point:
231	
232	- **Segmented header:** `Applications В· Submissions` (segmented control, not two tabs вҖ” keeps the 4-tab bar).
233	- **Default segment:** whichever has the higher unreviewed count; ties go to Submissions (higher creator-anxiety cost per day of delay).
234	- **Row grouping:** grouped by campaign with `SectionHeader` rows; tapping a row pushes into the campaign-scoped review screen (same route as В§2.3).
235	- **Empty state:** if both segments empty, "Nothing to review. Good time to post another bounty." with a CTA button to Create Campaign.
236	
237	So the campaign-scoped routes are the sources of truth; the Inbox tab is a lens. This matches how lister work actually happens вҖ” sometimes you think "that Nike campaign has applicants" (campaign-first), sometimes "do I have anything to review?" (inbox-first).

253	### 3.4 Deep Link Strategy
254	
255	Scheme: `marketify://`
256	
257	| Event | Deep link | Lands on | Back-stack seed |
258	|---|---|---|---|
259	| Creator app approved | `marketify://applications/:id` | Application Detail with animated status change | Applied tab вҶ’ Application Detail |
260	| Creator app rejected | `marketify://applications/:id` | Application Detail, rejection reason expanded | Applied tab вҶ’ Application Detail |
261	| Creator app cancelled (listing edited) | `marketify://applications/:id?reason=listing_edited` | Application Detail with "What changed" diff card | Applied tab вҶ’ Application Detail |
262	| Submission approved/rejected | `marketify://submissions/:id` | Submission Detail with status change | Submitted tab вҶ’ Submission Detail |
263	| Lister new application | `marketify://campaigns/:cid/applications/:aid` | Application Review | Home tab вҶ’ Campaign Detail (`:cid`) вҶ’ Applications Inbox вҶ’ Application Review |
264	| Lister new submission | `marketify://campaigns/:cid/submissions/:sid` | Submission Review | Home tab вҶ’ Campaign Detail (`:cid`) вҶ’ Submissions Inbox вҶ’ Submission Review |
265	| Metric refresh ready | `marketify://profile` | Profile with refresh banner | Profile tab (own root) |
266	
267	Built with `expo-router`. **Back-stack seeding rule:** deep links hydrate a realistic navigation spine, not just the target screen on top of the home tab. The table above spells out each link's spine вҖ” back-swipe from an Application Review lands the lister on the Applications Inbox they would have reached manually, not on Dashboard. One-level-deep links (creator Application Detail, Profile) seed a single parent tab and stop there.

431	**Sheet states** (internal state machine, not separate screens):
432	
433	| State | Visual | Transition |
434	|---|---|---|
435	| `input` | normal sheet, "Add handle" button enabled once @handle validates client-side | `POST /social-links` returns 201 вҶ’ `dispatching` |
436	| `dispatching` | "Add handle" becomes a `ButtonPrimary loading` with an inline spinner; input is read-only | edge function enqueues Apify runs вҶ’ `scraping` (usually <300ms) |
437	| `scraping` | sheet collapses to a pinned bottom progress banner (`MetricStaleIndicator state='refreshing'`, copy: "Fetching @sarahfilms вҖҰ") and Campaign Detail becomes interactive again; eligibility rows that depend on IG enter a `refreshing` visual | one or both IG webhooks land (`ig_details` / `ig_posts`) |
438	| `partial` (IG only) | banner shows "@sarahfilms В· followers вң“, views loadingвҖҰ"; eligibility rows that depend on `instagram_follower_count` can re-evaluate and pass / fail optimistically, while `instagram_avg_views_last_10`-dependent rows stay in `refreshing` | second webhook lands вҶ’ `fresh` or `failed` |
439	| `fresh` | banner flips to a 2.5s success toast ("@sarahfilms added вҖ” you're now eligible" / "вҖҰstill missing conditions"); eligibility rows re-render with final pass/fail | banner auto-dismisses; CTA re-evaluates; if now eligible the Apply CTA pulses once (`bouncy` spring) |
440	| `failed` | banner flips to `failed` MetricStaleIndicator variant with retry CTA: "Couldn't fetch @sarahfilms В· Edit handle" | tap вҶ’ re-opens AddHandleSheet in `input` state with handle pre-filled (doesn't unlink) |
441	| `scrape_error + handle_invalid` (distinct subkind вҖ” resolved from webhook's `error_message`) | banner copy: "@sarahfilms doesn't look like a real Instagram account. Double-check the spelling." | same as `failed` |
442	
443	**Interaction with В§5.6 "Is this you?" card.** If the creator adds a handle here and immediately navigates to Profile (e.g., from the "Edit Handles" secondary action later in the session), the first-scrape confirmation card renders on Profile *once the first `fresh` snapshot exists*. The two flows are independent вҖ” AddHandleSheet is about adding the handle for eligibility in context; the confirmation card is a profile-level sanity check. A creator who never visits Profile still sees the card the first time they do.
444	
445	**What the eligibility rows show during `scraping` / `partial`.**
446	- Rows bound to the being-scraped platform render `MetricStaleIndicator state='refreshing'` over their user_value (e.g., "12.3K" becomes a skeleton).
447	- The Apply CTA is disabled with copy: "Waiting for @sarahfilmsвҖҰ" вҖ” not "Not eligible" (honesty: eligibility is currently unknown, not failed).
448	- If the creator backgrounds the app and returns, the banner + refreshing state persist (driven by realtime subscription to `metric_snapshots` for the creator's own social_links).
449	- If the creator applies to a *different* campaign in the meantime, the banner follows them on that Campaign Detail too вҖ” it's a global pinned-bottom banner, not screen-local, until all outstanding runs resolve.
450	
451	**Backend contract.** The sheet calls `POST /social-links { platform, handle }` which (a) inserts `social_links` with `status='linked'`, (b) enqueues the scrape_modes for that platform via Apify client, (c) returns `{ social_link_id, queued_scrape_modes: [...] }` synchronously. The sheet then subscribes to realtime on `metric_snapshots` filtered to `social_link_id` and drives its state machine from webhook arrivals. The sheet is the only UI that interleaves a Campaign Detail eligibility check with an active scrape; the state model above is its spec.

603	Notes on the two Q-driven affordances:
604	- **ReuseBadge** reads from the `submission_reuse_view` server aggregate; tap opens a bottom sheet showing the other campaigns with their status so the lister can decide whether reuse is a problem for *their* brief (some briefs permit cross-posting, others require exclusivity).
605	- **OverrideEligibilityDialog** only appears when approving with failed post-conditions. Typed confirmation (not just a button) is intentional friction вҖ” overrides are auditable (`submissions.override_reason`, `submissions.override_by_user_id`) and we want the lister to pause. Reject-with-failed-conditions needs no dialog (expected path).
606	
607	**Self-affirm vs review checkbox mapping.** The Submission Composer (creator, В§4.4) and Submission Review (lister, В§4.6) render the same `ConditionChecklist` with different `mode` props:
608	- `mode='self'` (composer) вҶ’ two-state: `вҳҗ` unchecked / `вҳ‘` checked. Every row starts unchecked; the creator affirms each one. No `вң—` red state вҖ” a creator who can't honestly affirm a row just leaves it unchecked, and the client-side validator blocks submit until all rows are checked.
609	- `mode='review'` (lister) вҶ’ three-state: `вҳҗ` unreviewed / `вң“` passes / `вң—` fails. Rows land in unreviewed; the lister taps to cycle through the three states. `вң—` rows persist their red visual until decision, and light up the OverrideEligibilityDialog trigger on Approve.

613	## 5. Critical UX Moments
614	
615	### 5.1 Eligibility Failure (Soft Rejection)
616	
617	**Tone:** Never "you're not good enough." Always "here's what's missing, here's how to fix it."
618	
619	- **Copy:** "You're not eligible yet for this one."
620	- **Visual:** danger-soft background (warm, not aggressive red); danger icon (subtle circle-slash, not harsh X).
621	- **Always pair with action:** inline CTA per failing row ("Link Instagram", "Refresh metrics").
622	- **Motion:** failing rows fade in *after* passing rows (200ms delay), gentle single-pulse danger-soft background. No shaking.
623	- **Escape hatch:** "Notify me when I qualify" toggle at the bottom.
624	
625	### 5.2 Application Auto-Cancellation (Lister Edited Listing)
626	
627	This is a trust-critical moment.
628	
629	- **Notification copy:** "@cleanco updated Clean Beauty Review. Your application was cancelled вҖ” you can re-apply with the new terms."
630	- **Deep link:** Application Detail with a distinctive **"What changed" diff card** at top (cancelled-soft bg, cancelled-color 2px border, no shadow).
631	- **Diff card:** side-by-side old vs new for changed fields (price, pre/post-conditions). Changed values in primary-soft pills.
632	- **Primary CTA:** "Re-apply" (cta color) вҖ” takes creator to new campaign detail pre-scrolled to eligibility.
633	- **Secondary:** "Dismiss".
634	- **Motion:** status pill transitions pending/approved вҶ’ cancelled with 600ms color wash (no bounce вҖ” neutral).
635	- **Lister side:** "This will cancel **N** pending applications. They'll be notified." Requires explicit confirmation button.
636	- **Lister-side warning on subsequent edits:** after the first version bump, every subsequent edit confirmation also surfaces: *"Creators cancelled by a previous edit are **not** re-notified. Only currently active applications (N) will be told about this change."* This is a one-way fan-out вҖ” by design, to prevent spamming ex-applicants вҖ” but listers have expected "edit again = re-ping everyone" and been surprised. Copy is inline in the ConfirmationModal body, not a separate dialog, so it reads as context, not another obstacle.
637	
638	### 5.3 Submission Status Change (pending вҶ’ approved/rejected)
639	
640	The most emotional moment.
641	
642	**Approved:**
643	- Deep-link to Submission Detail.
644	- StatusPill: 200ms warning вҶ’ 320ms color-interpolated success + bouncy scale (1 вҶ’ 1.12 вҶ’ 1).
645	- Confetti-lite: 8 small 6Г—6 squares burst upward, fade over 800ms.
646	- Haptic: `Success`.
647	- Banner: "Approved вҖ” nice work!" cta-colored, auto-dismiss 2.5s.
648	- All respects `useReducedMotion`.
649	
650	**Rejected:**
651	- Same deep-link.
652	- StatusPill: 400ms warning вҶ’ danger wash + **one shake gesture made of 3 oscillation cycles** (Вұ4px each, 120ms per half-cycle, ~720ms total). No confetti.
653	- Haptic: `Warning` (not Error вҖ” not punitive).
654	- Lister feedback card always visible (danger-soft bg).
655	- **Primary CTA: "Find a similar bounty"** вҖ” routes to Feed pre-filtered by the rejected campaign's `listings.category` + a price band of Вұ30% around its `listings.price_cents`. Both columns exist in the schema (see `tech-architecture.md` В§7); the feed uses the `listings_category_price_idx` partial index for sub-ms response. The rejected path must offer forward motion, not just a closed door.
656	- **Secondary CTA: "Read campaign rules"** вҖ” re-orients toward learning for anyone who wants to understand the rejection.
657	- Copy under the feedback card: *"This one didn't land. Plenty more that fit your profile."* Blameless, forward-looking.
658	- Silent landing вҖ” no banner, no confetti anti-pattern.
659	
660	### 5.4 Metrics Refresh
661	
662	**State model is per-metric, not per-platform.** A single Instagram refresh is two independent Apify runs (follower count + avg views). They can land out-of-order, and one can fail while the other succeeds. TikTok is a single run and collapses to the simple case.
663	
664	**Per-metric states** (rendered by `MetricStaleIndicator`):
665	
666	| State | Visual | When |
667	|---|---|---|
668	| `fresh` | no chip; timestamp reads "Updated 2m ago" | last snapshot for this `scrape_mode` is `fresh` and `< 24h` old |
669	| `stale` | tiny warning-soft dot; timestamp reads "Updated 3d ago" | `fresh` but `> 24h` (auto-flipped by cron) |
670	| `refreshing` | faint pulsing skeleton over the metric value | a webhook is outstanding |
671	| `failed` | danger-soft chip: "Couldn't refresh В· Retry" | last snapshot is `failed`; tapping retries (respects 6h throttle) |
672	
673	**Pull-to-refresh gesture** on Profile:
674	- **Pull phase:** counter above metric tiles interpolates upward; "Release to refresh".
675	- **Release phase:** all per-metric tiles owned by the platform(s) being refreshed enter `refreshing` simultaneously. Per-platform odometer only starts when that mode's webhook lands.
676	- **Partial-success phase (IG):** if `ig_details` lands first, follower count runs the odometer + delta pill; `ig_avg_views_last_10` stays in `refreshing` with its skeleton until `ig_posts` lands. If `ig_posts` fails (e.g. photo-only profile), the views tile flips to `failed` with "Not enough video content" as the specific copy (we distinguish "no videos" from "scrape error" by checking `raw_payload`).
677	- **Full-success phase:** each mode's tile runs its own odometer with bouncy spring and fades its delta pill (`+1.2K`/`-300`) for 1.5s.
678	- **Error phase:** if a manual refresh hits the 6h throttle (`429 RATE_LIMIT`), toast surfaces the exact `retry_after_sec` using a **tiered format**:
679	  - `вүҘ 1h`: `"Try again in Xh Ym."` (e.g. "Try again in 4h 23m.")
680	  - `60s вүӨ t < 1h`: `"Try again in Xm."` (e.g. "Try again in 7m.")
681	  - `< 60s`: `"Try again in a few seconds."` (we round down aggressively here вҖ” nobody benefits from "try again in 11s")
682	  Never "in a minute" unless `retry_after_sec` literally falls between 60 and 119.
683	- **Last-refreshed timestamp** is per-platform, not per-metric, and reads the **older** of the two IG snapshots: "IG updated 14m ago" (not "5m ago" вҖ” honesty over recency).
684	
685	### 5.5 Empty State вҖ” No Eligible Campaigns
686	
687	- **Illustration:** geometric line art (primary + ink only).
688	- **Headline:** "Nothing matches you вҖ” yet."
689	- **Body:** "Campaigns get added daily. Meanwhile, here's what to try."
690	- **Two action cards:**
691	  - **"Refresh your metrics"** вҶ’ Profile, auto-triggers refresh.
692	  - **"See all campaigns"** вҶ’ flips eligibility toggle off, reloads feed.
693	- **Never** just "no results".
694	
695	### 5.6 First-Scrape Handle Confirmation ("Is this you?")
696	
697	Handles are self-declared вҖ” no OAuth, no ownership verification. That's fine for v1, but we want one *soft* confirmation step the first time a handle resolves to a real profile, so the creator catches their own typos (`@sarah.films` vs `@sarahfilms`) before we lean on the metrics for eligibility.
698	
699	- **When:** on the Profile screen, the first time *any* scrape for a newly-added handle lands with `status='fresh'` (not on re-scrapes, not on re-linked handles). Gated by `handle_confirmed_at` on `social_links`.
700	- **What renders:** an inline confirmation card at the top of Profile, *above* the metric tiles, with the scraped display name + avatar + the bio-first-line, and two buttons:
701	  - **"Yes, that's me"** вҶ’ sets `handle_confirmed_at = now()`, card fades out (220ms short).
702	  - **"Wrong profile"** вҶ’ opens Edit Handles with the handle pre-selected in the edit row. Doesn't un-link automatically вҖ” the creator re-types.
703	- **Tone:** neutral, not alarmed. Copy: *"Quick check вҖ” is this your account? We'll use these stats for campaign eligibility."*
704	- **Visual:** primary-soft background card, 2px ink border, hard shadow. Avatar is 1:1 with 2px border.
705	- **No blocking:** metric tiles render normally below the card. The creator can ignore it вҖ” the card just doesn't go away until they answer. Applications still work; the card is a nudge, not a gate.
706	- **Unconfirmed-handle footgun:** if a creator ignores this and we later detect their handle resolves to a suspiciously-different profile (e.g., metrics drop 90%+ between refreshes, bio changes language), we re-surface the card with danger-soft styling and slightly firmer copy вҖ” but this is a v2 concern; MVP just shows the card once per handle.
707	
708	---
709	
710	## 6. Component Library Inventory
711	
712	| Component | Purpose | Key props |
713	|---|---|---|
714	| **CampaignCard** | Feed + inbox cell | `title, company, platforms[], price, eligibility, variant: 'creator'\|'lister', counts?` |
715	| **StatusPill** | Status indicator | `status`, exposes `animateTo(newStatus)` worklet |
716	| **EligibilityBadge** | Compact badge on card | `eligible: boolean, reason?` |
717	| **EligibilityRow** | Single pre-condition row | `label, required, user_value, passed, action?` |
718	| **ConditionChecklist** | Post-condition list | `items[], mode: 'self'\|'review', onChange` |
719	| **MetricChip** | Follower / avg-views chip | `platform, label, value, delta?, lastUpdated?` |
720	| **VideoURLInput** | TikTok/IG URL with preview | `platform, onChange, onPreview` |
721	| **VideoThumbnail** | 9:16 or 1:1 preview | `url, aspect, onPress` |
722	| **ButtonPrimary** | CTA button | `label, onPress, loading, disabled, variant` |
723	| **ButtonSecondary** | Low-emphasis button | `label, onPress, loading, disabled` |
724	| **FAB** | Floating action (Lister) | `icon, onPress` |
725	| **Chip** | Filter + category | `label, active, variant, onPress` |
726	| **SectionHeader** | в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ Label в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җ | `title, action?` |
727	| **ProgressDots** | Wizard progress | `total, current` |
728	| **DiffCard** | "What changed" card | `oldValue, newValue, fieldName` |
729	| **EmptyState** | Generic empty | `illustration: EmptyStateIllustrationKey, title, body, primaryAction, secondaryAction?` вҖ” `EmptyStateIllustrationKey` is a string-keyed registry: `'no_campaigns' \| 'no_eligible' \| 'no_applications' \| 'no_submissions' \| 'lister_no_campaigns' \| 'lister_no_applications' \| 'lister_no_submissions' \| 'network_error' \| 'not_found'`. Each key maps to a ship-in-repo SVG (`assets/illustrations/<key>.svg`) using only `--primary` / `--cta` / `--ink` per В§1.5 |
730	| **ErrorState** | Recoverable error | `title, body, onRetry` |
731	| **SkeletonCard** | Loading placeholder | `height, shape` |
732	| **RefreshOdometer** | Number-scroll animation | `value, previousValue` |
733	| **Toast** | Top banner | `message, variant, duration` |
734	| **ConfirmationModal** | Two-button alert | `title, body, primaryLabel, onConfirm, variant` |
735	| **BottomSheet** | Filters + quick actions | `children, snapPoints` |
736	| **CelebrationBurst** | Approval confetti | `anchorRef` |
737	| **TabBar** | Custom tab bar per role | `tabs[], activeIndex` |
738	| **NotificationRow** | Inbox item | `type, title, timestamp, read, href` |
739	| **ReuseBadge** | "Also submitted to N other campaigns" chip on submission row / review sheet вҖ” surfaces cross-posting without blocking it | `count, variant: 'inline'\|'header'` |
740	| **OverrideEligibilityDialog** | Approve-time confirmation for listers overriding a failed post-condition; renders the failed-conditions summary + required typed confirmation | `failedConditions[], onConfirm, onCancel` |
741	| **MetricStaleIndicator** | Per-metric tri-state marker attached to `MetricChip` (fresh / stale / refreshing / failed) вҖ” drives the visual in В§5.4 | `state: 'fresh'\|'stale'\|'refreshing'\|'failed', retryAfterSec?, errorKind?: 'no_videos'\|'scrape_error', onRetry?` |
742	| **AddHandleSheet** | Bottom-sheet flow that adds a TikTok/IG handle inline from any surface (Campaign Detail eligibility row, Edit Handles). Owns its own state machine across `input вҶ’ dispatching вҶ’ scraping вҶ’ (partial) вҶ’ fresh \| failed` вҖ” see В§4.2 | `platform: 'tiktok'\|'instagram', preFilledHandle?: string, onSuccess: (socialLinkId) => void, onCancel: () => void` |
743	
744	---
745	
746	## 7. Anti-Patterns to Avoid
747	
748	- Heavy skeuomorphism.
749	- Emoji as UI icons.
750	- Soft blurred drop shadows вҖ” breaks the Neubrutalism contract.
751	- Scale-transform hover states that cause layout shift.
752	- Punitive copy around eligibility, rejection, or cancellation. Always blameless.
753	- 5-tab bars.
754	- Animated celebrations on the *rejected* path.
755	- Dense color coding without icons вҖ” every status uses color + icon + text.
756	- Hiding metric refresh as a tiny setting. Pull-to-refresh is the gesture users expect.
757	- Modal-over-modal.
758	
759	---
760	
761	## 8. Pre-Delivery Checklist
762	
763	- [ ] All SVG icons sourced from Lucide React Native, consistent 24Г—24 viewBox
764	- [ ] No emojis used as UI elements
765	- [ ] All `Pressable` components have press feedback
766	- [ ] Press/hover transitions 150вҖ“300ms
767	- [ ] Body text contrast verified вүҘ 4.5:1 (`#0F172A` on `#FFF9F2` = 17.8:1, AAA)
768	- [ ] Primary pink `#EC4899` never used as foreground text at body size (fails AAA at 3.3:1) вҖ” swap to `--primary-deep` `#831843` where text meaning is required
769	- [ ] All content text respects system Dynamic Type / font scale up to 130% (`maxFontSizeMultiplier = 1.3`); UI chrome (pills, tab labels, status chips) opts out with `allowFontScaling={false}`
770	- [ ] All `EmptyState` usages reference a key from the illustration registry; no loose illustration SVGs
771	- [ ] Focus states visible for external keyboard
772	- [ ] `useReducedMotion()` respected on all spring/shake/confetti
773	- [ ] Responsive across 360dp / 375pt / 390pt / 430pt
774	- [ ] Safe-area insets handled
775	- [ ] Color is never the sole status indicator
776	- [ ] All images have `accessibilityLabel`
777

content.content:
1	# Marketify вҖ” Technical Architecture Document
2	
3	**Scope:** Backend, data, APIs, workflows. UI/UX is separate (see `design.md`).
4	**Stack:** Expo + React Native + Reanimated + Supabase (Postgres, Storage, Edge Functions, Realtime) + **Apify** (two actors: `clockworks/tiktok-scraper`, `apify/instagram-scraper`).
5	**Posture:** Opinionated. Where the PRD is silent, a default is chosen with justification.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## 1. Design Decisions
10	
11	The table below captures every non-obvious architectural choice. Each row is a committed default that the rest of the document relies on вҖ” if you need to revisit one, expect ripple effects in В§4 (schema), В§5 (edge functions), and `design.md` (UX).
12	
13	### 1.1 Identity & Auth
14	
15	| Area | Decision |
16	|---|---|
17	| Metric collection | **Apify scrapers, no OAuth.** Creator signs up with role + username + TikTok handle and/or IG handle. Server kicks an Apify actor to fetch metrics asynchronously. Handle ownership is unverified in MVP. |
18	| Username uniqueness | `username` is the unique display identifier (citext). `(platform, handle)` is also unique across `social_links` вҖ” first creator to register a handle owns it. Collisions surface with a "handle already registered" error; we do not verify ownership. |
19	| Creator + lister on same account | One role per account. Dual-role users make two accounts. |
20	| Lister identity | Self-declared: username + email + org name. KYC is post-MVP. |
21	| Handle impersonation | No ownership verification in MVP вҖ” first registrant wins. Impersonation is a manual-report problem; accept until it becomes real abuse. |
22	
23	### 1.2 Listing Lifecycle
24	
25	| Area | Decision |
26	|---|---|
27	| States | `draft вҶ’ active вҶ’ (paused \| closed) вҶ’ archived`. No `expired` state. |
28	| Auto-expiration | None. `end_date` column retained for v1.1; listers close listings manually. |
29	| Submission cap | `max_submissions` nullable. Listers close manually when they're done вҖ” no auto-close trigger in v1. |
30	| Application uniqueness | One active application per `(creator, listing)`. Partial unique index. |
31	| Cascade-on-edit | Editing any versioned field вҖ” price, pre-conditions, post-conditions, sample videos, max_submissions вҖ” bumps a version and cascade-cancels pending applications with `cancelled_listing_edit`. Title/description edits do NOT cascade. |
32	
33	### 1.3 Applications & Submissions
34	
35	| Area | Decision |
36	|---|---|
37	| Withdraw | Creator can withdraw from `pending` вҶ’ `withdrawn`. |
38	| Eligibility drift between apply and approve | Re-check at approve-time. If now-ineligible, lister receives `409 INELIGIBLE_NOW` with `override_allowed=true`. No re-check at submit-time. |
39	| Videos per submission | 1..N video URLs per submission. `min_videos` post-condition (default 1). |
40	| Revision request | Binary approve/reject only. Column reserved for v1.1. |
41	| Reapplication | After `rejected` / `cancelled_listing_edit`: allowed if listing still active. After `withdrawn`: allowed immediately. |
42	| Submission reuse | A single video URL may be submitted to multiple campaigns. Surfaced to the lister via `ReuseBadge` (design В§4.6) but not blocked. |
43	| Disputes | Append-only `events` table; support email only in MVP. |
44	| Pending apps/submissions expiry | No auto-expire. Rows stay in-inbox until explicitly acted on. |
45	
46	### 1.4 Money
47	
48	| Area | Decision |
49	|---|---|
50	| Payments | No real money in MVP. `price` is display-only. Off-platform handshake. `payment_status` column stubbed for v2. |
51	
52	### 1.5 Notifications
53	
54	| Area | Decision |
55	|---|---|
56	| Events | Application approved/rejected/cancelled; submission approved/rejected; listing version changed; metrics refresh failed. |
57	| Channels | In-app inbox + Supabase Realtime. Push deferred to v1.1. |
58	| Transport | Realtime on `applications`, `submissions`, `notifications`. Poll fallback on app resume. |
59	
60	### 1.6 Metrics / Apify
61	
62	| Area | Decision |
63	|---|---|
64	| Rate limits | Apify costs real money per scrape. Server-side throttle: manual refresh once per 6h per `(user, platform)`. Background refresh daily via `pg_cron` for all linked handles with `fetched_at < now() - 24h`. |
65	| Scrape failures | `metric_snapshots.status = fresh \| stale \| refreshing \| failed`. "Outdated" badge if `fetched_at > 7d`. After 3 consecutive failed scrapes, `social_link.status = failed_fetch`, prompt creator to re-enter handle. |
66	| Video integrity | Validate via TikTok/IG oEmbed at submission time (`422` if 404). Nightly sweep re-validates `approved` submissions вҖ” flags but doesn't change state. |
67	| Post-submission performance | Out of MVP. Later: an Apify actor per submitted video to pull current view count. |
68	
69	### 1.7 Security / Abuse
70	
71	| Area | Decision |
72	|---|---|
73	| Spam/illegal listings | `report_listing` button вҶ’ admin table. Admin reviews via Supabase Studio. |
74	| Submission cross-posting | Flag via `ReuseBadge`, don't hard-block. |
75	
76	---
77	
78	## 2. State Machines
79	
80	Notation: `transition [actor: user|lister|system|time]`.
81	
82	### 2.1 Listing
83	
84	```
85	(init) в”Җв”Җcreate[lister]в”Җв”Җ> draft
86	draft в”Җв”Җpublish[lister]в”Җв”Җ> active
87	active в”Җв”Җpause[lister]в”Җв”Җ> paused
88	paused в”Җв”Җresume[lister]в”Җв”Җ> active
89	active в”Җв”Җclose[lister]в”Җв”Җ> closed
90	closed в”Җв”Җarchive[lister]в”Җв”Җ> archived
91	paused в”Җв”Җarchive[lister]в”Җв”Җ> archived
92	
93	Side-effect on edit mutating price/pre-cond/post-cond/samples/max_submissions while active:
94	  version++ AND cascade-cancel pending applications (see В§2.3)
95	```
96	
97	Terminal: `archived`.
98	
99	### 2.2 Application
100	
101	```
102	(init) в”Җв”Җsubmit[creator, gated by eligibility]в”Җв”Җ> pending
103	pending в”Җв”Җapprove[lister]в”Җв”Җ> approved
104	pending в”Җв”Җreject[lister]в”Җв”Җ> rejected
105	pending в”Җв”Җwithdraw[creator]в”Җв”Җ> withdrawn
106	pending в”Җв”Җlisting_version_bump[system]в”Җв”Җ> cancelled_listing_edit
107	pending в”Җв”Җlisting_archived[system]в”Җв”Җ> cancelled_listing_closed
108	approved в”Җв”Җlisting_archived[system]в”Җв”Җ> cancelled_listing_closed
109	```
110	
111	No time-based expiration in v1.
112	
113	### 2.3 Submission
114	
115	```
116	(init, requires application.status=approved) в”Җв”Җcreate[creator]в”Җв”Җ> pending
117	pending в”Җв”Җapprove[lister]в”Җв”Җ> approved
118	pending в”Җв”Җreject[lister]в”Җв”Җ> rejected
119	```
120	
121	No `revision_requested` in v1. No time-based expiration.
122	
123	### 2.4 Social Link
124	
125	```
126	(init) в”Җв”Җhandle_added[creator]в”Җв”Җ> linked       (triggers initial Apify scrape)
127	linked в”Җв”Җscrape_success[system]в”Җв”Җ> linked
128	linked в”Җв”Җscrape_fail x3[system]в”Җв”Җ> failed_fetch
129	failed_fetch в”Җв”Җhandle_reentered[creator]в”Җв”Җ> linked
130	linked в”Җв”Җunlink[creator]в”Җв”Җ> unlinked
131	```
132	
133	### 2.5 Metric Snapshot
134	
135	```
136	(init, on link or refresh) в”Җв”Җapify_run_started[system]в”Җв”Җ> refreshing
137	refreshing в”Җв”Җsuccess[system|apify_webhook]в”Җв”Җ> fresh
138	refreshing в”Җв”Җfail[system]в”Җв”Җ> failed
139	fresh в”Җв”Җage > 24h[time]в”Җв”Җ> stale
140	stale в”Җв”Җrun_started[creator_manual | system_daily]в”Җв”Җ> refreshing
141	failed в”Җв”Җretry[system|creator]в”Җв”Җ> refreshing
142	```
143	
144	Rule: eligibility filter uses `fresh` OR `stale`, never `failed`. UI warns if `stale`.
145	
146	---
147	
148	## 3. Apify Integration
149	
150	**Sources:**
151	- `apify-client` NPM package вҖ” [github.com/apify/apify-client-js](https://github.com/apify/apify-client-js)
152	- TikTok Scraper actor page вҖ” [apify.com/clockworks/tiktok-scraper](https://apify.com/clockworks/tiktok-scraper)
153	- Instagram Scraper actor page вҖ” [apify.com/apify/instagram-scraper](https://apify.com/apify/instagram-scraper)
154	
155	### 3a. Actor Catalog (v1)
156	
157	We use **two actors only**, consolidating all metric fetches:
158	
159	| Platform | Actor Slug | REST slug (with `~`) | Purpose |
160	|---|---|---|---|
161	| TikTok | `clockworks/tiktok-scraper` | `clockworks~tiktok-scraper` | Profile + last N videos in a single run |
162	| Instagram | `apify/instagram-scraper` | `apify~instagram-scraper` | Profile details **and** posts/reels вҖ” two runs per refresh (different `resultsType`) |
163	
164	**Actor endpoints** (both actors expose the same surface):
165	
166	- Sync (blocking, returns dataset items): `POST https://api.apify.com/v2/acts/<slug>/run-sync-get-dataset-items?token=<APIFY_TOKEN>`
167	- Async (returns run, webhook-completed): `POST https://api.apify.com/v2/acts/<slug>/runs?token=<APIFY_TOKEN>`
168	
169	In practice we use the `apify-client` JS SDK (`.actor(slug).call()` for sync, `.actor(slug).start()` for async) вҖ” it wraps both endpoints.
170	
171	### 3b. TikTok вҖ” `clockworks/tiktok-scraper`
172	
173	**Input (profile scrape):**
174	```json
175	{
176	  "profiles": ["<handle>"],
177	  "resultsPerPage": 10,
178	  "profileScrapeSections": ["videos"],
179	  "profileSorting": "latest",
180	  "excludePinnedPosts": true,
181	  "shouldDownloadVideos": false,
182	  "shouldDownloadCovers": false,
183	  "shouldDownloadSubtitles": false
184	}
185	```
186	
187	Notes:
188	- `profileSorting: "latest"` returns newest videos first вҖ” required so `avg_views_last_10` is the **recent** trailing window, not all-time popular.
189	- `excludePinnedPosts: true` avoids skewing the average with a single mega-viral pinned post.
190	- Download flags default to `true` on Apify; we override to `false` to cut run cost (we never need the media bytes).
191	
192	**Output** вҖ” one row per video, with the author profile **denormalized onto every row**. Relevant fields:
193	
194	| Path | Meaning | Maps to |
195	|---|---|---|
196	| `authorMeta.name` | handle | verification vs. input |
197	| `authorMeta.fans` | follower count | `follower_count` |
198	| `authorMeta.following` | following count | (stored for profile, not eligibility) |
199	| `authorMeta.heart` | total lifetime likes across videos | (stored) |
200	| `authorMeta.video` | total post count | `video_count` |
201	| `authorMeta.verified` | blue check | `is_verified` |
202	| `playCount` | **view count for this video** | contributes to `avg_views_last_10` |
203	| `diggCount` | like count for this video | (stored) |
204	| `shareCount`, `commentCount`, `collectCount` | engagement | (stored) |
205	| `videoMeta.duration` | seconds | (stored) |
206	| `createTimeISO`, `webVideoUrl` | timestamps / permalink | (stored) |
207	
208	**Metric derivation (TikTok):**
209	- `follower_count = items[0].authorMeta.fans`
210	- `avg_views_last_10 = mean(items[i].playCount for i in 0..min(10, len(items)))`
211	- `video_count = items[0].authorMeta.video`
212	
213	**One run per refresh.**
214	
215	### 3c. Instagram вҖ” `apify/instagram-scraper`
216	
217	The Instagram actor is a **multi-mode scraper**; what it returns depends on `resultsType`. For our use case we need two modes per refresh:
218	
219	**Run 1 вҖ” profile details (follower count):**
220	```json
221	{
222	  "directUrls": ["https://www.instagram.com/<handle>/"],
223	  "resultsType": "details",
224	  "resultsLimit": 1,
225	  "addParentData": false
226	}
227	```
228	
229	Output (one row, keyed on username):
230	
231	| Field | Meaning | Maps to |
232	|---|---|---|
233	| `username` | handle | verification |
234	| `followersCount` | follower count | `follower_count` |
235	| `followsCount` | following count | (stored) |
236	| `postsCount` | total posts | `media_count` |
237	| `fullName`, `biography`, `profilePicUrl` | profile chrome | (stored) |
238	| `isBusinessAccount` | is Business/Creator | (stored; informational only вҖ” **not required**) |
239	| `verified` | blue check | `is_verified` |
240	
241	**Run 2 вҖ” recent reels (view counts):**
242	```json
243	{
244	  "directUrls": ["https://www.instagram.com/<handle>/"],
245	  "resultsType": "posts",
246	  "resultsLimit": 10,
247	  "addParentData": false
248	}
249	```
250	
251	`resultsType: "posts"` returns the profile's recent feed (posts + reels interleaved). We filter client-side to `type === "Video"` entries and use their view counts.
252	
253	Output (one row per post) вҖ” relevant fields:
254	
255	| Field | Meaning | Notes |
256	|---|---|---|
257	| `type` | `"Image"` \| `"Video"` \| `"Sidecar"` | filter to `"Video"` |
258	| `videoPlayCount` | Instagram's "plays" metric | primary signal |
259	| `videoViewCount` | view metric (sometimes null on older posts) | fallback |
260	| `likesCount`, `commentsCount` | engagement | (stored) |
261	| `videoDuration` | seconds | (stored) |
262	| `caption`, `hashtags`, `mentions`, `timestamp`, `url`, `shortCode` | post meta | (stored) |
263	
264	**Metric derivation (Instagram):**
265	- `follower_count = details[0].followersCount`
266	- `media_count = details[0].postsCount`
267	- `avg_views_last_10 = mean(p.videoPlayCount ?? p.videoViewCount for p in posts where p.type === "Video")` вҖ” skip the profile entirely if there are fewer than 1 video posts (set `avg_views_last_10 = null`, surface in UI as "Not enough video posts to compute").
268	
269	**Photo-only creators:** if `posts` returns 0 `type === "Video"` entries across 10 results, we write `instagram_avg_views_last_10 = null` and surface it in the UI as "Not enough video posts to compute." **No fallback run to `resultsType: "reels"`** вҖ” the cost doubling (extra ~$0.007/refresh Г— every photo-only creator Г— daily cron) wasn't justified in back-of-envelope math vs. just telling the creator to post more reels. Listings that require `min_avg_views_last_n` on Instagram treat `null` as ineligible (fail-closed).
270	
271	**Two runs per IG refresh.** Budget: 1 run = TikTok refresh, 2 runs = IG refresh. No conditional third run.
272	
273	### 3d. Client Pattern
274	
275	```ts
276	import { ApifyClient } from 'apify-client';
277	const apify = new ApifyClient({ token: Deno.env.get('APIFY_TOKEN')! });
278	
279	// --- synchronous short-run (used on first signup вҖ” user is waiting) ---
280	const tiktok = await apify
281	  .actor('clockworks/tiktok-scraper')
282	  .call(
283	    {
284	      profiles: [handle],
285	      resultsPerPage: 10,
286	      profileScrapeSections: ['videos'],
287	      profileSorting: 'latest',
288	      excludePinnedPosts: true,
289	      shouldDownloadVideos: false,
290	      shouldDownloadCovers: false,
291	    },
292	    { waitSecs: 60 },
293	  );
294	const { items: tiktokItems } = await apify.dataset(tiktok.defaultDatasetId).listItems();
295	
296	// --- async run with webhook (used for cron refresh and initial IG scrapes) ---
297	const run = await apify
298	  .actor('apify/instagram-scraper')
299	  .start(
300	    {
301	      directUrls: [`https://www.instagram.com/${handle}/`],
302	      resultsType: 'details',
303	      resultsLimit: 1,
304	    },
305	    {
306	      webhooks: [{
307	        eventTypes: ['ACTOR.RUN.SUCCEEDED', 'ACTOR.RUN.FAILED', 'ACTOR.RUN.TIMED_OUT', 'ACTOR.RUN.ABORTED'],
308	        requestUrl: `${SUPABASE_URL}/functions/v1/webhooks-apify`,
309	        payloadTemplate: JSON.stringify({
310	          social_link_id: '<id>',
311	          scrape_mode: 'ig_details',       // or 'ig_posts' | 'tiktok_profile'
312	          run_id: '{{resource.id}}',
313	          status: '{{resource.status}}',
314	          dataset_id: '{{resource.defaultDatasetId}}',
315	        }),
316	        headersTemplate: JSON.stringify({
317	          'X-Apify-Webhook-Secret': Deno.env.get('APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET')!,
318	        }),
319	      }],
320	    },
321	  );
322	```
323	
324	**Signup flow (user waiting):** Use sync `.call(input, { waitSecs: 60 })` for the *first* scrape per handle. If the actor finishes inside 60s, populate the snapshot inline; if it doesn't, the `waitSecs` limit returns a `RUNNING` run and the webhook completes it later. UI shows "Fetching your metrics..." until either returns.
325	
326	**Cron refresh / manual refresh:** Always async (`.start()` with webhook). The refresh endpoint queues work and returns `202 Accepted` immediately.
327	
328	### 3e. Metric Derivation вҖ” Summary
329	
330	| Denormalized field on `creator_profiles` | TikTok source | Instagram source |
331	|---|---|---|
332	| `tiktok_follower_count` | `authorMeta.fans` | вҖ” |
333	| `tiktok_avg_views_last_10` | `mean(playCount[:10])` | вҖ” |
334	| `tiktok_video_count` | `authorMeta.video` | вҖ” |
335	| `tiktok_is_verified` | `authorMeta.verified` | вҖ” |
336	| `instagram_follower_count` | вҖ” | `details[0].followersCount` |
337	| `instagram_avg_views_last_10` | вҖ” | `mean(videoPlayCount ?? videoViewCount)` across `type === "Video"` posts |
338	| `instagram_media_count` | вҖ” | `details[0].postsCount` |
339	| `instagram_is_verified` | вҖ” | `details[0].verified` *(reserved; column add in v1.1)* |
340	
341	Stored fully in `metric_snapshots.raw_payload` (jsonb) for forensics and future backfills вҖ” if we later need `diggCount` trends we don't need to re-scrape.
342	
343	### 3f. Cost & Rate Management
344	
345	- Every actor run costs Apify credits. Published pricing at time of writing: TikTok Scraper вүҲ $0.40 / 1k videos (so ~$0.004 per 10-video run), Instagram Scraper вүҲ $2.30 / 1k results. Budget conservatively at ~$0.01 per TikTok refresh and ~$0.02 per IG refresh (details + 10 posts = 11 results).
346	- **Scale math (back-of-envelope):** 10k creators Г— daily cron refresh вүҲ $40/day TikTok-only, $200/day IG-only, $240/day fully-linked. That's ~$7.2k/month worst case. Sensitivity: cutting daily cron to every 48h halves that; capping free-tier creators to weekly refresh quarters it.
347	- **Hard throttle:** `metrics/refresh` server-side limit 1 per 6h per `(user, platform)`. `429` returned before any Apify call.
348	- **Daily cron enforcement:** `cron-refresh-metrics` edge function pulls `social_links` ordered by `metrics_fetched_at ASC NULLS FIRST` and keeps a running tally of `estimated_cost_usd`; it stops dispatching new runs once `estimated_cost_usd >= APIFY_DAILY_BUDGET_USD`. Skipped links get picked up first the next day because of the `NULLS FIRST` ordering and oldest-first tie-break. Estimate is static per `scrape_mode` (`tiktok_profile`: $0.005, `ig_details`: $0.005, `ig_posts`: $0.015) вҖ” no dynamic pricing lookup needed.
349	- Set Apify run timeout (via `timeoutSecs` input to `.start()`) to 120s; if actor hangs, we fail-close the snapshot (`status=failed`) and retry on next cron window.
350	- Memory: default `memoryMbytes` is fine for both actors (1024 MB); don't override.
351	
352	### 3g. Webhook Security
353	
354	- We attach `X-Apify-Webhook-Secret: <token>` via `headersTemplate` on every webhook registration. Edge Function rejects requests without a matching secret; compare via `crypto.subtle.timingSafeEqual`-equivalent (constant-time).
355	- Webhook endpoint is idempotent: `metric_snapshots.apify_run_id` has a partial unique index, so redelivery of the same `run_id` no-ops on the second write.
356	- Webhook event types registered: `ACTOR.RUN.SUCCEEDED`, `ACTOR.RUN.FAILED`, `ACTOR.RUN.TIMED_OUT`, `ACTOR.RUN.ABORTED`. All four resolve the snapshot (either `fresh` or `failed`).
357	
358	**Secret rotation procedure (dual-secret window):**
359	1. Edge function accepts EITHER `APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET` (current) OR `APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET_PREVIOUS` (grace). Both env vars are read and compared in constant time; either match succeeds.
360	2. Set `APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET_PREVIOUS = <current>`, then generate a new random 32-byte token and set `APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET = <new>`.
361	3. New Apify runs registered after this change carry the new secret in their `headersTemplate`. In-flight runs (registered with the previous secret) continue to deliver successfully because the edge function still accepts the previous secret.
362	4. After the longest possible run duration (we set `timeoutSecs: 120` + Apify processing вүӨ 10 min buffer = **15 minutes**), the janitor cron `fail-stuck-refreshing` will have fail-closed any surviving snapshots. Unset `APIFY_WEBHOOK_SECRET_PREVIOUS` to complete rotation.
363	
364	### 3h. Rendering Submitted Videos
365	
366	oEmbed for both:
367	- TikTok: `GET https://www.tiktok.com/oembed?url=<share_url>` вҶ’ `html`, `thumbnail_url`.
368	- Instagram: `GET https://graph.facebook.com/v<version>/instagram_oembed?url=<reel_url>&access_token=<app_token>` вҶ’ thumbnail + attribution. (Note: this needs an app-level Meta token, NOT a creator token; that's still required for oEmbed but is a one-time app setup, not per-user OAuth.)
369	- Alternative: construct TikTok embed URL `https://www.tiktok.com/embed/v2/<video_id>` directly.
370	
371	---
372	
373	## 4. Supabase Database Schema
374	
375	Applied from `supabase-postgres-best-practices`: RLS enabled on all public tables; FKs indexed; partial indexes for hot filters; `security_invoker` on views; `security definer` functions in private schema.
376	
377	### 4.1 Tables Overview
378	
379	| Table | Purpose |
380	|---|---|
381	| `users` | Unified account row; role-gated. |
382	| `creator_profiles` | 1:1 extension; denormalized metrics for fast filtering. |
383	| `lister_profiles` | 1:1 extension for lister fields. |
384	| `social_links` | Creator's linked handles (TikTok and/or IG). No tokens вҖ” we don't OAuth. |
385	| `metric_snapshots` | Historized metric data per social_link, one Apify run per row. |
386	| `listings` | Core campaign row; mutable fields. |
387	| `listing_versions` | Immutable versioned snapshots. |
388	| `listing_conditions` | Pre + post conditions, structured rows. |
389	| `sample_videos` | Sample video URLs per version. |
390	| `applications` | Creator вҶ’ listing_version. |
391	| `submissions` | Deliverables on an approved application. |
392	| `submission_videos` | N video URLs per submission. |
393	| `notifications` | In-app inbox. |
394	| `push_tokens` | Expo push (v1.1). |
395	| `events` | Append-only audit log. |
396	
397	*(`oauth_states` is gone вҖ” no OAuth.)*
398	
399	### 4.2 Enums
400	
401	```sql
402	create type user_role as enum ('creator', 'lister');
403	create type platform as enum ('tiktok', 'instagram');
404	create type listing_status as enum ('draft', 'active', 'paused', 'closed', 'archived');
405	create type application_status as enum (
406	  'pending', 'approved', 'rejected', 'withdrawn',
407	  'cancelled_listing_edit', 'cancelled_listing_closed'
408	);
409	create type submission_status as enum ('pending', 'approved', 'rejected');
410	create type social_link_status as enum ('linked', 'unlinked', 'failed_fetch');
411	create type metric_status as enum ('fresh', 'stale', 'refreshing', 'failed');
412	-- Which Apify actor run produced this snapshot. TikTok has one mode; IG has two (details + posts).
413	create type scrape_mode as enum ('tiktok_profile', 'ig_details', 'ig_posts');
414	create type condition_kind as enum ('pre', 'post');
415	create type condition_metric as enum (
416	  'min_followers', 'min_avg_views_last_n', 'min_total_likes',
417	  'min_videos_posted', 'verified_only',
418	  'post_min_video_duration_sec', 'post_max_video_duration_sec',
419	  'post_min_video_count', 'post_must_mention', 'post_family_friendly',
420	  'post_must_tag_account'
421	);
422	create type notification_kind as enum (
423	  'application_approved', 'application_rejected', 'application_cancelled',
424	  'submission_received', 'submission_approved', 'submission_rejected',
425	  'listing_version_changed', 'metrics_refresh_failed'
426	);
427	```
428	
429	### 4.3 Denormalization
430	
431	- `creator_profiles.tiktok_follower_count`, `instagram_follower_count`, `_avg_views_last_10`, `metrics_fetched_at` вҖ” denormalized from latest `metric_snapshots` via trigger. Enables O(log n) feed filtering.
432	- `listings.current_version_id` вҖ” FK to `listing_versions`, avoids feed join.
433	- `listings.approved_submissions_count`, `active_pending_applications_count` вҖ” trigger-maintained counters.
434	- `applications.listing_version_id` вҖ” makes cascade a single UPDATE.
435	- `listings.min_followers_tiktok`, `min_followers_ig` вҖ” generated columns (MAX across pre-conditions per platform), btree-indexed for feed filter.
436	
437	### 4.4 RLS Model
438	
439	Custom JWT issued by our `/auth/*` edge functions (signed with project JWT secret). Payload:
440	```json
441	{ "sub": "<user_id>", "role": "creator|lister", "session_id": "<uuid>" }
442	```
443	RLS policies read `auth.jwt() ->> 'sub'`. JWT is signed by us, not user-editable. Safe for authz.
444	
445	### 4.5 Indexes
446	
447	- Feed: partial index `(status, created_at DESC) WHERE status='active'`.
448	- Eligibility pre-filter: generated columns `listings.min_followers_tiktok`, `min_followers_ig` btree-indexed.
449	- Application uniqueness: partial unique `(listing_id, creator_id) WHERE status in ('pending','approved')`.
450	- Notifications: `(user_id, read_at NULLS FIRST, created_at DESC)`.
451	
452	### 4.6 Triggers / Functions
453	
454	All in `app_private` schema (security definer).
455	
456	| Function | Trigger |
457	|---|---|
458	| `bump_listing_version()` | BEFORE UPDATE on `listings` вҖ” if versioned field changed, INSERT new version, cascade-cancel pending apps, emit notifications. |
459	| `denorm_metrics()` | AFTER INSERT on `metric_snapshots` where `is_latest=true` вҖ” updates `creator_profiles`. |
460	| `submission_side_effects()` | AFTER UPDATE on `submissions` when status вҶ’ `approved` вҖ” increments count. (Auto-close-on-max deferred to v1.1.) |
461	| `emit_event()` | Helper called by state-changing functions. |
462	
463	### 4.7 Full Migration SQL
464

478	-- =========================================================
479	-- ... (as above)

486	  role       user_role not null,

489	  created_at timestamptz not null default now(),

1747	### 5.6 Applications
1748	
1749	**`POST /applications`**
1750	```ts
1751	interface Req { listing_id: string; cover_note?: string; }
1752	interface Res { application_id: string; listing_version_id: string; }
1753	```
1754	Server re-evaluates eligibility; notifies lister.
1755	Errors: `403 INELIGIBLE`, `409 ALREADY_APPLIED`, `409 LISTING_NOT_ACTIVE`, `409 LISTING_VERSION_CHANGED`.
1756	
1757	**Concurrency contract вҖ” closes the race with `bump_listing_version`.** Without row locks, an applicant's `INSERT applications` can interleave with a lister's `UPDATE listings` that triggers the version bump + cascade-cancel, leaving a fresh `pending` row pinned to an already-stale version. The insert txn must serialize on the listing row:
1758	
1759	```sql
1760	begin;
1761	-- 1. Pin the listing to this txn. Both POST /applications and the edge-function
1762	--    wrapper of bump_listing_version must take this lock. The bump trigger runs
1763	--    under the UPDATE's own row lock, so they are mutually exclusive.
1764	select id, status, current_version_id
1765	  from public.listings
1766	 where id = :listing_id
1767	 for update;
1768	
1769	-- 2. Abort if the listing moved out of 'active' in that window.
1770	-- 3. Re-run eligibility against the NOW-current version_id (not the one the
1771	--    client thought was current when it opened the confirm sheet).
1772	-- 4. Insert applications.listing_version_id = the locked current_version_id.
1773	commit;
1774	```
1775	
1776	If the lister's version bump commits first, the creator's txn reads the new `current_version_id` after acquiring the lock; the eligibility rules may now differ, so the edge function re-checks and returns `409 LISTING_VERSION_CHANGED` with the new version's diff, which the client surfaces as "Terms just changed вҖ” review before applying" on Campaign Detail. No silent commit against a stale version is possible.

1815	### 5.9 Realtime Channels
1816	
1817	| Channel | Who | Events |
1818	|---|---|---|
1819	| `notifications:user_id=<me>` | both | INSERT |
1820	| `applications:listing_id=<id>` | lister | INSERT, UPDATE |
1821	| `submissions:listing_id=<id>` | lister | INSERT, UPDATE |

Channel filters in the client are an optimization, not a security boundary. Manual test plan for launch: subscribe from Creator A's JWT to a channel filtered on Creator B's id, perform a write against Creator B's data, assert zero events delivered. 1	# Ralph Progress Log вҖ” Marketify
2	Started: (will be replaced on first run)
3	---
4	
5	## Codebase Patterns
6	*Append-only consolidated learnings. Each future iteration reads this BEFORE doing anything.*
7	
8	- **Authoritative specs are `docs/product-plan.md`, `docs/design.md`, `docs/tech-architecture.md`.** When the story is ambiguous, read the relevant section there. Do not invent product behavior.
9	- **Use Supabase MCP, not the supabase CLI**, for migrations, queries, edge functions, and type generation. Migrations are append-only and named `us_NNN_description`.
10	- **Use mobile-mcp for any UI verification.** Never claim a UI story is done without a screenshot from `mobile_take_screenshot`.
11	- **Use the `feature-dev:code-reviewer` subagent before every commit.** It catches drift across iterations that you can't see in your fresh context.
12	- **Delegate exploration.** If a story would have you read >3 files, spawn the `Explore` subagent first and work from its summary.
13	- **Always regenerate `src/types/supabase.ts`** after any schema migration. Commit the regenerated file in the same commit as the migration.
14	- **Use `bun`, not npm/yarn/pnpm.** It is what's installed locally.
15	- **JWT secret is `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET`**, separate from Supabase's built-in JWT secret. We sign our own per docs/product-plan.md В§2.
16	- **Apify key is `APIFY_KEY`** in `.env`. Read via `process.env.APIFY_KEY` or `Deno.env.get("APIFY_KEY")`. Never paste into source.
17	- **Edge functions live in `supabase/functions/<name>/index.ts`** with shared utils under `supabase/functions/_shared/`.
18	- **Component organization:** primitives in `src/components/primitives/`, screen-specific in `src/screens/<screen>/`, shared composites in `src/components/shared/`.
19	- **No emoji in product UI** вҖ” Lucide icons only (per docs/design.md).
20	- **One role per account in v1** вҖ” no dual-role logic.
21	- **Run `get_advisors({type:'security'})` after every RLS or schema change.** Address every error-level finding before commit.
22	- **`expo lint` default glob is `['src', 'app', 'components']`.** Empty subdirs with only `.gitkeep` cause eslint to exit 2 ("all files ignored"). Keep at least one lintable `.ts`/`.tsx` file in any of those roots (`src/index.ts` with `export {};` was used for US-001).
23	- **Expo bootstrap workaround:** `create-expo-app .` refuses to run when the target dir has dotfiles/dirs it considers hostile. Bootstrap into a sibling folder (e.g. `.expo-init`), then move the generated files up. Preserves `.claude/`, `.env`, `docs/`, `scripts/`.
24	- **Reanimated 4.x babel plugin is `react-native-worklets/plugin`** (not the legacy `react-native-reanimated/plugin`). It lives in the `react-native-worklets` package вҖ” pin it explicitly via `bunx expo install react-native-worklets` so `bun install --production` won't drop it. Included by default in fresh Expo SDK 54 templates.
25	- **Prefer `bunx expo install <pkg>`** over `bun add` for native modules вҖ” it pins the SDK-compatible version. Expo's install runs `bun add` under the hood when a `bun.lock` is present.
26	- **App entry is `"main": "expo-router/entry"`** in package.json. The old `index.ts` + `App.tsx` from US-001 were removed. Root layout lives at `app/_layout.tsx`; initial route at `app/index.tsx`.
27	- **Expo Go + SDK 54 + new-arch + `<Stack>` from expo-router throws at runtime**: `Exception in HostFunction: TypeError: expected dynamic type 'boolean', but had type 'string'`. For US-002 the root uses `<Slot />` as the minimal root. US-031 will revisit with a dev-client build (`expo run:ios`) where Stack + Tabs should work.
28	- **iOS simulator boot flow for mobile-mcp verification:** `xcrun simctl boot <name>` вҶ’ `open -a Simulator` вҶ’ `bunx expo start --ios` in background вҶ’ `xcrun simctl openurl <udid> exp://127.0.0.1:8081` if Expo Go doesn't auto-open the project. mobile-mcp's `mobile_open_url` blocks `exp://` schemes by default, so shell out.
29	- **Supabase project:** `marketify` (ref=`[REDACTED_SUPABASE_PROJECT_REF]`, region=`ap-southeast-1`). All MCP DB calls use this `project_id`. Pre-installed extensions: pgcrypto, uuid-ossp, pg_stat_statements, supabase_vault, pg_graphql. pg_cron + citext available on request.
30	- **Install extensions `with schema extensions`** (e.g. `create extension if not exists citext with schema extensions`) to keep advisors clean. `pg_cron` is the exception: Supabase requires it in `pg_catalog`, leave it unqualified.
31	- **Enum canonical list is docs/tech-architecture.md В§4.2** (lines 399-427). After generating types, verify `src/types/supabase.ts` Constants block matches it exactly.
32	- **Local migration files live under `supabase/migrations/` with `YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_us_NNN_description.sql`.** Keep filename and MCP migration `name` (no timestamp prefix) consistent. Commit every applied migration вҖ” `list_migrations` is the DB side, the file is the source of truth for fresh applies.
33	- **`app_private` schema was created in US-003** via a companion migration (`us_003_app_private_schema`). Revoke is already in place for `anon` and `authenticated`. Triggers / security-definer functions in later stories should target this schema.
34	- **Reference `citext` as `extensions.citext` in every migration.** US-003 installed it with `with schema extensions`; the default `search_path` used by migrations does not include that schema, so an unqualified `citext` fails to resolve.
35	- **Supabase auto-enables RLS on new `public.*` tables** on this project. New table migrations will show up in `get_advisors({type:'security'})` as INFO-level `rls_enabled_no_policy` (not `rls_disabled`). These INFO findings are benign until policies land in US-009 вҖ” no commit-blocking action needed.
36	- **`listing_conditions` is a single data-driven table**, not separate pre/post tables. Discriminated by `condition_kind` enum ('pre' | 'post') per docs/tech-architecture.md В§4.7 block 8. Pre-condition rows use metrics like `min_followers`; post-condition rows use `post_*` metrics. The story US-005 text named separate tables but the spec wins.
37	- **Circular FK pattern:** `listings.current_version_id` references `listing_versions(id)` but both tables are in the same migration. Create the `listings` table with the column nullable, create `listing_versions`, then `alter table public.listings add constraint ... foreign key ...`. Spec follows this pattern (В§4.7 lines 659-661).
38	- **Supabase defaults views to `security_invoker=off` (effectively DEFINER)**; it will fail the `security_definer_view` advisor. Every view we create must be followed with `alter view <name> set (security_invoker = on)` to match the project standard at В§4.1 line 375. Applied to `submission_reuse_view` in US-006.
39	- **RLS helper pattern (use for every policy that scopes to the caller):** two SQL `stable` helpers `public.current_user_id()` (returns `nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'sub', '')::uuid`) and `public.current_user_role()` (returns `nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'role', '')::public.user_role`). Both use `set search_path = ''` and schema-qualify every ref. `nullif` on the empty string makes a missing claim safely return NULL (all `id = current_user_id()` checks fail closed); for `current_user_role()` this is a hardening deviation from the spec's verbatim `(auth.jwt() ->> 'role')::user_role` вҖ” intentional, keeps the fn from raising on malformed JWTs. Policies call these helpers instead of inlining `auth.jwt()` for readability + single-source-of-truth. Ref: `supabase/migrations/20260417140000_us_009_rls_policies.sql`.
40	- **The `submission_reuse_view` вҶ’ `submission_reuse_count(uuid)` function swap (US-009 resolution):** the view with `security_invoker=on` would be filtered by the caller's `submissions_lister_read` policy and hide cross-lister reuses (the ReuseBadge's entire purpose). The fix: **drop the view** and replace with a SECURITY DEFINER function `public.submission_reuse_count(p_submission_id uuid) returns integer` that (1) does its own ownership check via join through `submissions вҶ’ applications вҶ’ listings.lister_id = current_user_id()`, (2) returns NULL when the caller isn't the listing owner, (3) bypasses RLS to count cross-lister reuses. Revoked from public, granted to authenticated + service_role. Keep this pattern for any other cross-tenant aggregate the UI needs.
41	- **Verify RLS via a transactional `begin; ... rollback;` block:** seed fresh fixtures with known `auth.uid`s, then `set local role authenticated` + `set local "request.jwt.claims" = '{"sub":"<uuid>","role":"creator"}'` and assert row visibility via `insert into _asserts ...` rows that you `select *` at the end before rolling back. Important: `grant insert, select on _asserts to authenticated` before switching roles, else you'll get `permission denied for table _asserts`. `PERFORM` is PL/pgSQL only вҖ” don't use it outside a DO block. Check `information_schema.columns` before writing fixtures so INSERTs match real schema (columns migrate between blocks вҖ” e.g. `listing_version_id` lives on `applications`, not `submissions`).
42	- **Trigger function hardening pattern (mandatory for every new trigger / PL/pgSQL function in this repo):**
43	  1. Declare `set search_path = ''` on the function (CREATE OR REPLACE drops prior SET clauses, so restate it on every redefinition).
44	  2. With empty `search_path`, qualify EVERY user-defined ref: tables `public.<tbl>`, types/enum casts `public.<type>` (e.g. `'tiktok'::public.platform`), `app_private.<fn>` for sibling functions.
45	  3. For pg_catalog **functions** (`now()`, `hashtextextended()`, `pg_advisory_xact_lock()`, etc.): bare is fine вҖ” `pg_catalog` is always implicitly searched even with empty `search_path`. Schema-qualifying as `pg_catalog.<fn>()` also works for true functions.
46	  4. **GREATEST / LEAST / COALESCE / NULLIF are SQL parser constructs, NOT functions.** They CANNOT be schema-qualified (`pg_catalog.greatest()` raises 42883). Always write them bare.
47	  5. SECURITY DEFINER functions especially require empty `search_path` вҖ” without it, a caller-controlled search_path can shadow `public.<table>` and divert writes. The advisor `function_search_path_mutable` flags this at WARN.
48	- **US-008 TODO вҖ” out-of-order Apify webhook delivery:** `app_private.denorm_metrics` uses `greatest(metrics_fetched_at, new.fetched_at)` to guard the timestamp column, but the surrounding column UPDATEs (e.g. `tiktok_follower_count`) are unconditional. If a webhook with an older `fetched_at` lands after a newer one (different `apify_run_id`, so the dedup index doesn't catch it), the trigger will overwrite fresh denorm columns with stale values even though the timestamp itself refuses to roll back. The spec В§15 has the same gap. Resolution when US-008 (apify-core / webhook receiver) lands: either add `where new.fetched_at >= public.creator_profiles.metrics_fetched_at` to each branch's UPDATE, or guard the whole denorm block on a max(fetched_at) check inside the advisory lock. Update the spec section in the same PR.
49	- **US-008 nice-to-have вҖ” advisory lock collision risk:** `app_private.denorm_metrics` uses single-arg `pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtextextended(...))`. Two distinct (social_link_id, scrape_mode) strings can collide to the same 64-bit key, causing unrelated creators to serialize unnecessarily. Not a correctness issue (only throughput). Standard fix is the two-arg form `pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtext(social_link_id::text), hashtext(scrape_mode::text))`. Spec В§15 codifies the single-arg form; address as a spec amendment when load matters.
50	- **`public.events` append-only is a social contract, not a DB-level guarantee.** US-008 reproduces the spec literally (`revoke all on public.events from anon, authenticated`), but `service_role` and `postgres` retain every grant including UPDATE/DELETE вҖ” by design per spec В§4.4 lines 1083вҖ“1085 ("Service role only. No application read/write policy"). Any edge function or trigger that writes to `events` must treat INSERT as the only safe verb; there is no DB trigger that blocks UPDATE/DELETE. If stronger enforcement is ever desired, add a BEFORE UPDATE OR DELETE trigger raising `RAISE EXCEPTION` (this would also block maintenance scripts вҖ” considered too restrictive for v1).
51	- **In PL/pgSQL with `set search_path = ''`, `text[] || 'literal'` resolves to `anyarray || anyarray` and crashes with `22P02 malformed array literal`.** Postgres chooses the array+array overload for unknown-type literals. Always cast the right-hand side: `changed_fields || 'price_cents'::text`. Typed text columns (e.g. `new.version_bump_reason`) work bare because the operator resolves to `anyarray || anyelement` when the right side has a concrete element type. This gotcha is independent of the `public.<type>` schema-qualification pattern already in Codebase Patterns. Resolved in US-010 via `us_010_fix_array_append` follow-up migration.
